title: mentha arvensis var. nemorum
intro: Mentha arvensis var. nemorum, also known as Corn Mint or Field Mint, is a subspecies of mint plant that is native to Europe. It is a perennial herb that has been traditionally used for its medicinal and culinary properties. The plant is known for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Corn Mint can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote digestive health, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
